description: This figure presents the density functional theory calculated variations of two mechanical parameters of Fe₁₋ₓMnₓAl alloys as a function of Mn content (x ranging from 0 to 1.0). The red square curve denotes Young's modulus (E) with the unit GPa, corresponding to the left y-axis, while the blue square curve represents the elastic constant difference C₁₁-C₁₂, corresponding to the right y-axis. Both parameters follow a highly similar variation trend: they first decrease with increasing Mn content from ~296 GPa (E) and ~160 (C₁₁-C₁₂) at x=0, reach the first minimum at x=0.5 (~200 GPa for E, ~72 for C₁₁-C₁₂), then rise to a peak at x=0.8 (~228 GPa for E, ~115 for C₁₁-C₁₂), and finally drop to the lowest value at x=1.0 (~178 GPa for E, ~52 for C₁₁-C₁₂). The consistent variation trend verifies the correlation between Young's modulus and elastic constants of the alloy system, and supports the conclusion that Fe₀.₅Mn₀.₅Al possesses the optimal ductility among the studied Fe-Mn-Al alloys.
